session.name: Video workspace trim/split — Phase A (viewer corner chips)

goal.original: Implement the video workspace trim + split controls plan (docs/plans/2026-05-14-video-workspace-trim-split-controls.md). Start with Phase A and proceed phase-by-phase.
goal.summary: Phase A complete: MpiViewerCorners Compound built, image workspace's hard-coded compare overlay migrated onto it. Chip strip now matches mockup — single dark box with border, dot-separated uppercase items, accent-heat for hover/active. Next: Phase B (MpiTrimBar Compound, isolated, dev-gallery validated).

completed: Phase A.1 — Create MpiViewerCorners Compound (js/css), register in preloadStyles, document in types.js, mount on dev gallery with two chips

Phase A.2 — Migrate MpiCanvasViewer __compare-overlay markup onto MpiViewerCorners; preserve public API (setCompareEnabled, setActiveToolLabel, compare-clicked emit); delete obsolete mpi-canvas-viewer__compare* CSS

Visual polish after user feedback — single shared box per mockup (oklch(0.30 0.02 350 / 0.85), border --line, 6px 12px padding, t-2xs/0.18em), dot separators auto-inserted between chips, accent uses --accent-heat
pending: Phase B.1 — Create MpiTrimBar Compound (js/components/Compounds/MpiTrimBar/{js,css}). Props: duration, fps, value (playhead seconds), inPoint, outPoint. Three draggable handles (in/out/playhead) + range fill, overflow ±8px, frame-snap, RAF/mouseup seek coalescing, public setValueQuiet/setRangeQuiet, on()/off() unsubs + el.destroy(). Register CSS, document in types.js, mount on dev gallery with duration: 14.74, fps: 30, inPoint: 1.0, outPoint: 12.5
Phase C — Split MpiVideoPlayer into MpiVideoSurface + MpiVideoControlBar; reshape MpiVideoViewer
Phase D — Trim persistence (per-sidecar atomic writes) + hotkeys (I/O/X)
Phase E — Range-aware ops (loop-within-range, frame-step clamp, snapshot, extend, crop export)
Parallel Batch — chip content for video workspace + viewer bg + control-bar polish
Phase G — Wrapper cleanup, delete MpiVideoPlayer

completed: Plan file ## Completed section updated with Phase A.1 + A.2 entries
Plan file Phase A todo checkboxes marked [x]
Kanban entry moved PLANNING → IMPLEMENTING with Phase A status note + next-step pointer to Phase B
pending: Per CLAUDE.md cardinal rule 3: after Phase G ships, ask user about updating .claude/rules/component-mounts.md / component-events.md / component-state.md / components.md (§ Stage design baseline) to reflect the split + corner chip primitive
After Phase D, consider feedback_item_sidecar_atomic_writes.md memory (only if a new per-sidecar queue helper is introduced)
After Phase E, consider feedback_trim_range_ops.md memory capturing 'ops always read out when range set' convention

next_action.description: Begin Phase B.1: build MpiTrimBar Compound in isolation. Create js/components/Compounds/MpiTrimBar/MpiTrimBar.{js,css}. Props duration/fps/value/inPoint/outPoint, three draggable elements (in handle, out handle, playhead) plus track + range fill, overflow handles ±8px top/bottom with end-caps per editor-video.html mockup, frame-snap on fps, RAF or mouseup seek-commit (per feedback_chromium_seek_coalescing memory), constraints 0 ≤ in ≤ playhead ≤ out ≤ duration, setValueQuiet/setRangeQuiet for external sync, on()/off() helpers + el.destroy. Register css in preloadStyles.js, document props in types.js, mount on dev gallery (tpl-components.html + js/pages/components.js) with duration: 14.74, fps: 30, inPoint: 1.0, outPoint: 12.5 to verify before any wiring into video workspace.

key_decisions: Chip strip style: matched editor-video.html .ratio-readout pattern — single span-style box, oklch(0.30 0.02 350 / 0.85) background, 1px --line border, 6px 12px padding, t-2xs font + 0.18em tracking, dots inserted between items. User rejected earlier per-chip pill style and earlier flat-text-no-box style; this is the third iteration that finally matches the mockup.
Accent color = --accent-heat (red) for hover/active chips, NOT --ink-1. Confirmed against mockup screenshot.
Tool-label chip text no longer manually appends ' ·' — separator is auto-rendered between chips by the component. Adjusted in MpiCanvasViewer setActiveToolLabel path.
Hide-when-empty: topRightEl.style.display flips none/'' based on _items.length so an empty chip set leaves no visible empty box.
Public viewer API preserved unchanged so MpiGroupHistoryBlock and other callers need zero changes from Phase A.
constraints: Stage tokens only — no hardcoded hex. New border/background uses oklch literal because the mockup itself uses one (no token exists yet for this surface tint).
ComponentFactory.create() pattern; el.destroy() collects unsubs; MpiCanvasViewer.destroy now also calls _cornersInst.el.destroy().
Phase B MpiTrimBar must follow feedback_chromium_seek_coalescing.md (coalesce playhead-drag seeks on RAF or commit only on mouseup — do NOT write video.currentTime on every pixel).
Phase B is isolated — no wiring into video workspace yet, dev-gallery validation only.
MpiVideoPlayer monolith stays untouched until Phase C (split) and Phase G (deletion). Dev gallery still mounts the old MpiVideoPlayer for now.

js/components/Organisms/MpiCanvasViewer/MpiCanvasViewer.css (removed all .mpi-canvas-viewer__compare* rules; added small __corners host rule)
.claude/mpi-kanban/kanban.md (moved trim entry PLANNING → IMPLEMENTING, added Phase A status body + Phase B pointer)

js/components/Compounds/MpiVideoPlayer/MpiVideoPlayer.js (current monolith — for seek+loop dance lines 247-262 and frame-step wrap lines 296-320; Phase B's MpiTrimBar drives the surface that will eventually replace this)
js/components/Primitives/MpiProgressBar/MpiProgressBar.js (single-handle reference; MpiTrimBar is custom two-handle + playhead from scratch)

rules_active: .claude/rules/components.md (§ Stage design baseline — BEM, factory, destroy contract)
.claude/rules/events.md (component-local emit vs global Events bus — MpiTrimBar emits component-local seek/in-change/out-change/range-change)
.claude/rules/state.md (Phase D only — trim sidecar field via update-meta)
resume_prompt: You are resuming an in-flight implementation of the video workspace trim + split controls plan (docs/plans/2026-05-14-video-workspace-trim-split-controls.md, kanban entry 'Video workspace trim + split controls' in IMPLEMENTING). Phase A is complete — MpiViewerCorners Compound exists at js/components/Compounds/MpiViewerCorners/ and MpiCanvasViewer's compare overlay now routes through it with chip styling matching docs/redesign/c-stage/editor-video.html (single dark box with --line border, dot separators, --accent-heat for active). Read docs/handoffs/2026-05-14-14-30-video-trim-phase-a.json for full context, then read the plan file's Phase B section and editor-video.html for the trim-bar mockup. Run /mpi-kanban:mpi-continue and begin Phase B.1: build MpiTrimBar Compound in isolation (js/components/Compounds/MpiTrimBar/{js,css}) with props duration/fps/value/inPoint/outPoint, three draggable handles + range fill, frame-snap, RAF/mouseup seek coalescing per feedback_chromium_seek_coalescing memory, component-local seek/in-change/out-change/range-change emits, setValueQuiet/setRangeQuiet, on()/off() unsubs + el.destroy. Register CSS in preloadStyles.js, document props in types.js, mount on dev gallery (tpl-components.html + js/pages/components.js) with duration: 14.74, fps: 30, inPoint: 1.0, outPoint: 12.5. Do not wire into the video workspace yet — that is Phase C. Do not commit; user runs commits manually.
